ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Yong Tau Foo; FOODON_03302051 (stuffed food product) is the closest defensible parent class. Laksa broth variant with 7 boiled pieces is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dishes — mixed dishes with vegetables and protein (boiled/soup-based)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (composite mixed dish) is the closest defensible anchor. No specific Yong Tau Foo laksa code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for Yong Tau Foo or laksa-based stuffed tofu dish.

serving.description: 7 pieces of assorted stuffed yong tau foo items in laksa broth
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of yong tau foo laksa consists of 7 assorted boiled stuffed items (approximately 350 g total including broth), served in a spiced coconut milk laksa soup at a hawker stall.

nutrient_source.title: Yong Tau Foo (Laksa, 7 pieces, boiled) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for Yong Tau Foo (Laksa, 7 pieces, boiled), CR ID F08010065. All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this canonical Singapore government source. Saturated fat of 3.90 g/100 g is estimated from coconut milk composition typical of laksa broth and is consistent with HPB data patterns for similar dishes; it is not explicitly stated in the HPB source record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Vegetable and Tofu Dishes — analogous estimate based on boiled stuffed tofu and low-GI vegetable components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yong Tau Foo laksa in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 45 (low) is estimated by analogy with boiled tofu-based mixed dishes and low-GI vegetables (eggplant, bitter gourd, okra) which individually score 15–54 in the Sydney database. Coconut milk laksa broth contributes fat that further attenuates glycaemic response. Glycemic load per 100 g calculated as (45 × 4.66) / 100 ≈ 2.1, which is retained as the per-100 g GL figure.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 464 mg/100 g; laksa broth from shrimp paste and seasoning is a major sodium contributor
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the laksa broth and focus on the solid stuffed pieces, as the sodium content of 464 mg/100 g (approximately 1,624 mg per 350 g serving) substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg per meal threshold; requesting a reduced-sodium broth or consuming only half the broth can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (4.66 g/100 g) and low GI (45); protein moderates glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Yong tau foo laksa (7 pieces, boiled) is suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes when consumed without added rice or noodles, as the low carbohydrate load (approximately 16 g per 350 g serving), low GI of 45, and moderate protein (4.07 g/100 g) collectively produce a minimal glycaemic impact; however, the coconut milk broth adds saturated fat and should be consumed in moderation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.9 g/100 g) from coconut milk laksa broth and elevated sodium (464 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit broth consumption due to the saturated fat content (~3.9 g/100 g) derived from coconut milk, which can raise LDL cholesterol, and the high sodium (464 mg/100 g) which contributes to blood pressure elevation; focusing on the boiled stuffed pieces and requesting a lighter broth reduces both saturated fat and sodium exposure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk, elevated sodium, and nightshade vegetables (eggplant, chili) may promote inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should exercise caution with this dish as the coconut milk broth contributes pro-inflammatory saturated fat (~3.9 g/100 g), sodium at 464 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold, and nightshade vegetables such as eggplant and chili contain solanine alkaloids that may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yong Tau Foo with Laksa Broth (7 pieces, boiled) is a popular Singaporean hawker dish consisting of seven assorted stuffed items — typically including tofu puffs, bitter gourd, eggplant, chili, lady's finger (okra), and fish-paste-filled tofu — served in a rich, spiced coconut milk laksa broth. The dish originates from Hakka Chinese culinary tradition and has been deeply integrated into Singaporean and Peranakan food culture. The boiled preparation keeps the stuffed pieces tender and moist, while the laksa broth — made with coconut milk, shrimp paste (belacan), lemongrass, galangal, and dried chili — imparts a creamy, aromatic, mildly spicy flavour. Per 100 g, the dish provides 91 kcal, 4.07 g protein, 6.23 g fat (predominantly saturated fat from coconut milk at approximately 3.9 g/100 g), and 4.66 g carbohydrate with 0.5 g dietary fibre and 2.08 g sugar. Sodium is notably high at 464 mg/100 g, primarily from shrimp paste and seasoning in the broth. The glycaemic index is estimated at 45 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management when consumed without rice or noodles. Clinically, the dish is appropriate for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its low carbohydrate and low GI profile, but is a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to elevated sodium and saturated fat from the coconut milk broth. It is not suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to the combination of saturated fat, high sodium, and nightshade vegetables. The dish is a staple at Singapore hawker centres and food courts, commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner meal.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (CR ID F08010065), which is the canonical primary source for Singapore hawker dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are exact matches. Saturated fat estimated at ~3.9 g/100 g based on coconut milk composition typical of laksa broth, consistent with HPB data patterns. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable boiled tofu and vegetable dish profiles.
notes: G3 SENTINEL VALUE DOCUMENTATION: The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because the HPB Foodo database entry F08010065 for Yong Tau Foo (Laksa, 7 pieces, boiled) does not report these values, and USDA FoodData Central does not contain an equivalent composite Singapore hawker dish entry from which these values could be reliably extracted as of 2026. Source consulted and confirmed unavailable: HPB Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/, accessed 2026-04-16) and USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/, accessed 2026-04-16). Fields set to -1: monounsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported in HPB Foodo for this composite dish; polyunsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported in HPB Foodo for this composite dish; trans_fat_g — trans fat content not reported in HPB Foodo for this composite dish; potassium_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; calcium_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; iron_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; cholesterol_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_c_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_b6_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_b12_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; folate_mcg_dfe — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_d_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; vitamin_e_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; phosphorus_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; magnesium_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; zinc_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; selenium_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065; water_g — moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo entry F08010065. Saturated fat of 3.90 g/100 g is estimated from the known coconut milk composition in laksa broth and is consistent with HPB data patterns for similar dishes; it is not explicitly stated in the HPB source record. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using boiled tofu-based mixed vegetable dishes; no direct GI measurement for Yong Tau Foo laksa exists in the database. The dietary_tag 'vegetarian' in the input is incorrect as the dish contains fish paste and shrimp paste; this has been corrected to is_vegetarian: false. The dietary_tag 'keto' in the input is borderline; fat is 6.23 g/100 g and carbohydrate is 4.66 g/100 g, which is consistent with low-carb but the coconut milk broth volume makes strict keto classification uncertain; this flag has been omitted from dietary_flags. Halal status is marked false as shrimp paste (belacan) preparation and sourcing vary; consumers should verify halal certification at point of purchase.
